author: harjeb
text: Implemented support/benchmark_3x3_schemes.cpp to run doc schemes A-E against five valid 9R demo boards. Verified with MSYS2 g++ because cmake and msbuild are unavailable. Current results: A/B/C/E solve near-complete boards; D solves only easiest within limits; scattered/harder boards time out or A prototype blocks due missing full 15-puzzle macro handling.

author: harjeb
text: Verification commands: (1) PATH prepended with C:/msys64/ucrt64/bin, g++ -std=c++14 -O2 support/benchmark_3x3_schemes.cpp -o build/benchmark_3x3_schemes.exe; build/benchmark_3x3_schemes.exe passed and produced A-E timing results. (2) Existing core test g++ -std=c++14 -O2 -Iinclude support/test.cpp src/pazusoba.cpp -o build/test_pazusoba.exe compiled but failed at support/test.cpp:346; no core files were changed.

author: harjeb
text: Updated benchmark demos to valid boards with exactly 9 R orbs and no horizontal/vertical 3-match for any color. Added runtime validation has_initial_line3(). Raised B/C/D/E per-scheme search limits to 10s. Results: only C beam solved one legal board (cost 11) in 21 steps / 7448.6ms; A prototype failed on blank path blocking; B A* timed out; D GRASP failed; E timed out then A fallback failed.
